name: tesseract-olap
documentation: ## Installation Besides the main contents of the package, you can install the optional dependencies for the backend driver of your choice: * `tesseract-olap[clickhouse]` Installs the dependency needed to enable the use of the `tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse` module. ## Getting started In its most basic form, the tesseract-olap package provides you with a way to translate OLAP-type queries into request statements that a data backend can understand and execute safely. The results obtained through the execution of server methods are python objects, and as such, can be used in any way the language allows. ```python # example.py from tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse import ClickhouseBackend from tesseract_olap import OlapServer backend = ClickhouseBackend("clickhouse://user:pass@localhost:9000/database") server = OlapServer(backend=backend, schema="./path/to/schema.xml") def get_data(): # First you create an ordered representation of the intent for data request = DataRequest.new("cube_name", { "drilldowns": ["Time", "Country"], "measures": ["Units", "Price"], }) # This step performs the validation of the request against the schema query = DataQuery.from_request(server.schema, request) # The context manager establishes the connection with the backend with server.session() as session: # .fetch() methods perform the request against the server. # There are three methods depending on the shape you want the data: # result = session.fetch(query) # result = session.fetch_dataframe(query) result = session.fetch_records(query) return result.data if __name__ == "__main__": get_data() ``` The server instance can then be used in other programs as the data provider, for simple (like data exploration) and complex (like data processing) operations. --- © 2022 [Datawheel, LLC.](https://www.datawheel.us/) This project is licensed under [MIT](./LICENSE).

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.enums.Comparison
documentation:
Comparison Enum.

Defines the available value comparison operations.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.enums.JoinType
documentation: Defines the different types of join operations available to the user.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.enums.LogicOperator
documentation:
Logical connector Enum.

Defines logical operations between conditional predicates.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.enums.Membership
documentation:
Membership Enum.

Defines the membership of a value to a set.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.enums.NullityOperator
documentation:
Nullity correspondence Enum.

Evaluates if a value is or not is NULL.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.enums.Order
documentation:
Order direction Enum.

Defines a direction to use in a sorting operation.

modules.name: tesseract_olap.query.models
documentation:
Query-related internal structs module.

This module contains data-storing structs, used mainly on the query and backend
modules.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.CutIntent
documentation:
Filtering instructions for a qualitative value.

Instances of this class are used to define cut parameters.
Its values are directly inputted by the user, so should never be considered
valid by itself.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.FilterIntent
documentation:
Filtering instructions for a quantitative value.

Instances of this class are used to define filter parameters.
Its values are directly inputted by the user, so should never be considered
valid by itself.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.JoinIntent
documentation: Specifies the intent of the user to perform a Join operation between 2 datasets.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.PaginationIntent
documentation: Pagination instructions.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.SortingIntent
documentation: Sorting instructions for internal use.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.TimeRestriction
documentation:
Time-axis filtering instructions for internal use.

Instances of this class are used to define a time restriction over the
resulting data. It must always contain both fields.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.TopkIntent
documentation:
Limits the results to the K first/last elements in subsets determined by
one or more levels and their associated value. Adds a column that indicates
the position of each element in that ranking.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.GrowthIntent
documentation: Calculation of growth with respect to a time parameter and a measure

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.HierarchyField

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.HierarchyField.alias
documentation: Returns a deterministic unique short ID for the entity.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.HierarchyField.deepest_level
documentation:
Returns the deepest LevelField requested in this Hierarchy, for this
query operation.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.HierarchyField.foreign_key
documentation:
Returns the column in the fact table of the Cube this Dimension
belongs to, that matches the primary key of the items in the dim_table.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.HierarchyField.has_drilldowns
documentation:
Verifies if any of the contained LevelFields is being used as a
drilldown.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.HierarchyField.primary_key
documentation:
Returns the column in the dimension table for the parent Dimension,
which is used as primary key for the whole set of levels in the chosen
Hierarchy.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.HierarchyField.table
documentation:
Returns the table to use as source for the Dimension data. If not
set, the data is stored directly in the fact table for the Cube.

Contains the parameters associated to a slicing operation on the data,
based on a single Hierarchy from a Cube's Dimension.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.LevelField

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.LevelField.alias
documentation: Returns a deterministic unique short ID for the entity.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.LevelField.iter_columns
documentation:
Generates triads of (column name, column alias, pair hash) for all fields related to
a HierarchyField object.

This comprises Drilldown Labels and IDs, and its requested Properties.

Contains the parameters associated to the slice operation, specifying the
columns each resulting group should provide to the output data.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.MeasureField

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.MeasureField.alias_name
documentation: Returns a deterministic short hash of the name of the entity.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.MeasureField.alias_key
documentation: Returns a deterministic hash of the key column of the entity.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.MeasureField.name
documentation: Quick method to return the measure name.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.MeasureField.aggregator_params
documentation: Quick method to retrieve the measure aggregator params.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.MeasureField.aggregator_type
documentation: Quick method to retrieve the measure aggregator type.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.models.MeasureField.get_source
documentation: Quick method to obtain the source information of the measure.

MeasureField dataclass.

Contains the parameters needed to filter the data points returned by the
query operation from the server.

modules.name: tesseract_olap.query.queries
documentation:
Query-related data-handling models module.

This module contains data structs used to carry and compose objects used during
a Query. The elements are agnostic to the type of backend used, and its primary
purpose is organize and easily obtain the data needed for later steps.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.query.queries._get_data_hierarfields
documentation: Regroups query parameters related to a Level, to simplify later usage.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.query.queries._get_data_measurefields
documentation: Regroups query parameters related to a Measure, to simplify contextual use.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.query.queries._get_members_hierarfield
documentation: Regroups query parameters related to a Level, to simplify later usage.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.queries.DataQuery

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.queries.DataQuery.from_request
documentation:
Generates a new {class}`Query` instance from the parameters defined
in a {class}`DataRequest` object.

If any of the parameters can't be found on the Schema, raises a derivate
of the {class}`InvalidQuery` error.

Internal DataQuery class.

Contains all the schema-hydrated elements corresponding to a
{class}`DataRequest`, but also joining properties related to the same
columnar entities.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.queries.DataMultiQuery.from_requests
documentation:
Validates the DataRequests into DataQueries and reorganizes the JoinIntent operations that are intended to be performed with each of them.

By itself this class is not intended to be sent to the backend for execution (as the join operation is performed using DataFrames), but in the future and depending on the Backend implementation, it's subject to change.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.queries.MembersQuery

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.queries.MembersQuery.from_request
documentation:
Generates a new {class}`MembersQuery` instance from a user-provided
{class}`MembersRequest` instance.
Internal MembersQuery class.

modules.name: tesseract_olap.common
documentation: This module defines common objects, shared by the entire library.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.common.strings.get_localization
documentation:
Attempts to return the value from a dictionary of terms, where the locale
code is the key.

If it doesn't find the specific locale, looks for the general locale code,
and if it's not available either, returns the value for the default locale.

modules.name: tesseract_olap.query.requests
documentation:
Requests module.

Contains structs to build a {class}`DataRequest` instance: an object to describe
the parameters needed for the query using only entity names and relationships.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.requests.RequestWithRoles
documentation: Defines an interface of commons between DataRequest and MembersRequest.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.requests.DataRequestOptionalParams
documentation:
Defines the optional parameters in the DataRequestParams interface.

Is a separate class is due to the implementation of the
\[Totality\](<https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0589/#totality>) in the
{class}`TypedDict` class.

This will give a better hint to the type checker when the user makes use of
this interface.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.requests.DataRequestParams
documentation:
DataRequestParams interface.

Determines the expected params in a {class}`dict`, to use when creating a
new {class}`DataRequest` object via the {func}`DataRequest.new` class method.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.requests.DataRequest

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.requests.DataRequest.new
documentation:
Creates a new {class}`DataRequest` instance from a set of parameters
defined in a dict.

This should be the preferred method by final users, as it doesn't
require the use of internal dataclasses and the setup of internal
structures and unique conditions.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.requests.DataRequest.key
documentation:
Generates a hash to differentiate the parameters that influence the resulting data.

This hash can be used to compare requests, and as cache key for the resulting data.
It doesn't consider roles on purpose, as the roles define the access to the dataset
instead of its contents. This also means a comparison operation between requests must
compare roles separately.

Represents the intent for a Data Query made by the user.

All its properties are defined by strings of the names of the components
from the schema.
None of these parameters are verified during construction, so it's possible
for the query to be invalid; a subclass of {class}`backend.exceptions.BackendError`
will be raised in that case.
The only purpose of this structure is containing and passing over the query
intent to the internals.

During a request, a {class}`Query` instance is constructed with objects from
a schema, using parameters from this instance.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.requests.MembersRequestOptionalParams
documentation:
Defines the optional parameters in the MembersRequestParams interface.

Is a separate class is due to the implementation of the
\[Totality\](<https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0589/#totality>) in the
{class}`TypedDict` class.

This will give a better hint to the type checker when the user makes use of
this interface.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.requests.MembersRequestParams
documentation:
MembersRequestParams interface.

Determines the expected params in a {class}`dict`, to use when creating a
new {class}`MembersRequest` object via the {func}`MembersRequest.new` class
method.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.query.requests.MembersRequest

methods.name: tesseract_olap.query.requests.MembersRequest.new
documentation:
Creates a new {class}`MembersRequest` instance from a set of parameters
defined in a dict.

This should be the preferred method by final users, as it doesn't
require the use of internal dataclasses and the setup of internal
structures and unique conditions.

Represents the intent for a Level Metadata Query made by the user.

Parameters are constructed with primitives that describe the entities being
requested.

It is suggested to use the {func}`MembersRequest.new` method to create a new
instance of this class, instead of calling a new instance directly.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.server.UnknownBackendError
documentation:
This error occurs when the user attempts to initialize the Server with
a connection string that doesn't match any known compatible Backend.

The user must be informed to ensure they're using a compatible database.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.server.UnknownSchemaError
documentation:
This error occurs when the user attempts to point the location of the
Schema to a file in an unknown format.

The user must be informed that by default, only XML and JSON files are
allowed.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.server.server._setup_backend
documentation:
Generates a new instance of a backend bundled in this package, or raises
an error if no one is compatible, with a provided connection string.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.server.server._setup_cache
documentation: Generates a new instance of a CacheProvider bundled in this package.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.server.server.OlapServer

methods.name: tesseract_olap.server.server.OlapServer.raw_schema
documentation: Retrieves the raw Schema instance used by this server.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.server.server.OlapServer.build_query
documentation:
Uses the internal schema to validate a Request and generate its
matching Query instance.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.server.server.OlapServer.clear_cache
documentation: Clears all stored items in the cache.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.server.server.OlapServer.generate_sql
documentation:
Builds the SQL query for a Query instance, using the backend currently
configured in this server.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.server.server.OlapServer.ping
documentation:
Performs a ping call to the backend server.
A succesful call should make this function return {bool}`True`.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.server.server.OlapServer.validate
documentation:
Verifies the information declared in the Schema matches the data
structures in the Backend.

Main server class.

This object manages the connection with the backend database and the schema
instance containing the database references, to enable make queries against
them.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.server.schema.setup_schema
documentation:
Generates a new Schema instance from a string source.

The source can be a path to a local file, an URL to an external schema file,
or the text content of a schema file to be parsed.
Raises a {class}`ValueError` instance if the source can't be recognized as
either of these.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.server.schema._parse_source_url
documentation: Attempts to fetch a remote URL and parse its contents as a Schema.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.server.schema._parse_source_raw_string
documentation: Attempts to parse the contents of the provided string as a Schema.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.server.schema._parse_path_file
documentation: Attempts parse the contents of a reference to a local file as a Schema.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.TesseractError
documentation: Base class for exceptions in `tesseract_olap` module.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.BackendError
documentation: Base class for exceptions in `tesseract_olap.backend` module.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.QueryError
documentation: Base class for exceptions in `tesseract_olap.query` module.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.SchemaError
documentation: Base class for exceptions in `tesseract_olap.schema` module.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.ServerError
documentation: Base class for exceptions in `tesseract_olap.server` module.

modules.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.query
documentation:
Query exceptions module.

The errors in this module refer to problems during the retrieval of data from
the backend, but happening at the core side of the code.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.query.InvalidQuery
documentation:
This error occurs when a query is misconstructed.

Can be raised before or after a request is made against a data server.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.query.InvalidParameter
documentation:
This error occurs when a parameter in the query is incorrectly set before
any data validation happens. This includes parameters with the wrong type.

As this is entirely an user issue, should be informed to the user, but not
necessarily to the administrator.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.query.InvalidEntityName
documentation: This error occurs when a query asks for an object missing in the schema.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.query.TimeDimensionUnavailable
documentation:
This error occurs when a query asks for a time restriction, but the Cube
doesn't contain a Dimension whose type is "time".

On an information level this should be informed to the user, and in a debug
level should be reported to the administrator to check if there's a issue
with the schema.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.query.TimeScaleUnavailable
documentation:
This error occurs when a query asks for a specific time granularity which
is not configured in the levels of any hierarchy in the selected dimension.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.query.InvalidFormat
documentation:
This error occurs when a format used to retrieve data is not supported by
the upstream server.

Should be raised before the query is executed against the upstream server.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.query.MissingMeasures
documentation:
This error occurs when a measure is being specified in part of the query,
but it's not included in the list of measures for the query.

Should be raised before the query is executed against the upstream server.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.query.NotAuthorized
documentation:
The roles provided don't match the roles needed to access some of the
requested parameters.

Should be raised before the query is executed against the upstream server.

modules.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.backend
documentation:
Backend exceptions module.

The errors in this module refer to problems during the retrieval of data from
the backend, but happening at the backend plugin side of the code.
These should be extended/raised from inside the Backend module.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.backend.UpstreamNotPrepared
documentation:
This error happens when there's a connection attempt done against a
backend which is unable to accept connections.

The user must avoid race conditions and ensure connections and cursors are
in usable state.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.backend.UpstreamInternalError
documentation:
This error occurs when a remote server returns a valid response, but the
contents give details about an internal server error.

This must be caught and reported to the administrator.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.backend.UnexpectedResponseError
documentation:
This error occurs when the response sent by the remote server doesn't
adjust to the expected shape, and the database driver has problems to handle
it.

This must be caught and logged, but usually is related to a malformed query,
so not further handling might be needed.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.backend.BackendValidationError
documentation:
This error occurs when the schema is configured to use certain features
that the backend server, or the backend module, is not prepared to handle.

This must be reported to the administrator, but also should stop the process
so the user can fix the schema.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.backend.BackendLimitsException
documentation:
This exception happens when the user intends to perform a request that
exceeds the capabilities of the Backend to handle the data.

The Backend preemptively cancels the request, and must inform the user so
the request can be reformulated.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.schema.XMLParseError
documentation: An error happened while trying to parse a XML Schema.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.schema.MalformedXML
documentation: An unexpected node was found.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.schema.InvalidXMLAttributeName
documentation: An invalid attribute was found in a node.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.schema.InvalidXMLAttributeValue
documentation: An invalid value was found in the attribute of a node.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.schema.MissingXMLNode
documentation: A required child node is missing.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.schema.MissingXMLAttribute
documentation: A required attribute is not present.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.schema.JSONParseError
documentation: An error happened while trying to parse a JSON Schema.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.schema.MalformedJSON
documentation: An unexpected object was found.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.schema.MissingPropertyError
documentation:
A mandatory property couldn't be retrieved from a Shared/Usage entity
combination.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.schema.InvalidNameError
documentation: The name of an Entity contains invalid characters.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.schema.DuplicateKeyError
documentation: The key of some property in the schema is shared in two nodes.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.schema.DuplicatedNameError
documentation: The name of an Entity is duplicated across its parent cube.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.exceptions.schema.EntityUsageError
documentation:
There's a declared Usage reference pointing to a non-existent shared
Entity.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.cursor.fetch_membercount
documentation: Threaded function to request member count for all levels in cube.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.cursor.inyect_members_count
documentation:
Updates the `count` property on all Levels in the Schema with the number of members, as returned by the provided cursors from the ClickhouseBackend.

The process runs in parallel using as many cursors are passed as argument.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.cursor.validate_schema_tables
documentation: Validates the declared set of table, columns in the Schema entities with the ones available in the Backend.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.dialect.TypedDictCursor
documentation:
A cursor that generates results as {class}`dict`.

`fetch*()` methods will return dicts instead of tuples.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.dialect.ClickhouseDataType

methods.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.dialect.ClickhouseDataType.from_membertype
documentation: Transforms a MemberType enum value into a ClickhouseDataType.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.dialect.ClickhouseDataType.to_membertype
documentation: Transforms a ClickhouseDataType enum value into a MemberType.

Lists the types of the data the user can expect to find in the associated
column.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.backend.ClickhouseBackend

methods.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.backend.ClickhouseBackend.ping
documentation: Checks if the current connection is working correctly.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.backend.ClickhouseBackend.validate_schema
documentation:
Checks all the tables and columns referenced in the schema exist in
the backend.

Clickhouse Backend class

This is the main implementation for Clickhouse of the core {class}`Backend`
class.

Must be initialized with a connection string with the parameters for the
Clickhouse database. Then must be connected before used to execute queries,
and must be closed after finishing use.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.backend.dataframe.generate_lag_column
documentation:
Generates a pl.Expr that represents the same series used as reference,
but with each value lagged by delta units.

modules.name: tesseract_olap.backend.models
documentation:
Backend model definitions module.

This module contains abstract definitions for the interfaces of the Backend
class. Tesseract is compatible with any kind of data source as long as there's a
backend class that adapts the Query and the Results to the defined interface.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.backend.models.Backend

methods.name: tesseract_olap.backend.models.Backend.new_session
documentation:
Establishes the connection to the backend server.

This operation must be done before running any other data method, and
must be separate from the creation of a {class}`Backend` instance.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.backend.models.Backend.ping
documentation:
Performs a ping call to the backend server.
If the call is successful, this function should return {bool}`True`.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.backend.models.Backend.validate_schema
documentation:
Ensures all columns defined in the schema are present in the backend.
Should raise an Error if it finds any problem, otherwise should return `None`.
Base class for database backends compatible with Tesseract.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.backend.models.Session

methods.name: tesseract_olap.backend.models.Session.connect
documentation:
Establishes the connection to the backend server.

This operation is called automatically when the Session instance is
used within a context manager.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.backend.models.Session.close
documentation:
Closes the connection to the backend server.

This operation is called automatically at the end of the context manager
this instance was called into.
Base class for connections made to a backend compatible with Tesseract.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.backend.models.ParamManager

methods.name: tesseract_olap.backend.models.ParamManager.set_param
documentation: Stores a new named parameter value, and returns the parameter name.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.backend.models.ParamManager.set_table
documentation: Stores an inline table.

Keeps track of the SQL named parameters and their values, to combine them
through all the functions where they're defined, and output them at the
final generation step.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.backend.cache.CacheProvider
documentation: Base class for the implementation of a cache layer for the Backend.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.backend.cache.CacheConnection
documentation: Internal Base class for individual connections to the cache layer.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.backend.cache.DummyProvider
documentation: A CacheProvider used when the user doesn't set a valid one. Will always MISS.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.backend.cache.DummyConnection
documentation: The CacheConnection associated to DummyProvider. Will always MISS.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.backend.cache.LfuProvider
documentation: Stores elements in a dictionary under the Least Frequently Used caching strategy.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.backend.cache.LfuConnection
documentation: The CacheConnection associated to LfuProvider.

modules.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.sqlbuild
documentation:
ClickHouse SQL generation module.

Comprises all the functions which generate SQL code, through the pypika library.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.sqlbuild.dataquery_sql
documentation:
Build the query which will retrieve an aggregated dataset from the
database.

The construction of this query has two main parts:
- The Core Query,
    which retrieves all the columns needed for later steps, and applies the
    filter on the qualitative fields (cuts)
- The Grouping Query,
    which applies the calculations/aggregations over the data, filters on
    quantitative fields (filters), applies pagination, sorting and the
    aliases over the columns

The returned query is composed by the Grouping query on the Core query as
subquery.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.sqlbuild.membersquery_sql
documentation:
Build the query which will list all the members of a Level in a dimension
table.

Depending on the filtering parameters set by the user, this list can also
be limited by pagination, search terms, or members observed in a fact table.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.sqlbuild._get_aggregate
documentation:
Generates an AggregateFunction instance from a measure, including all its
parameters, to be used in the SQL query.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.backend.clickhouse.sqlbuild._get_filter_criterion
documentation: Apply comparison filters to query

modules.name: tesseract_olap.schema.aggregators
documentation:
Schema Aggregators module.

Contains the different types of aggregator that can be used in the definition of
a Measure in the Schema.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.schema.aggregators._get_fields
documentation: Returns a set of strings, with the names of the fields in this class.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.schema.aggregators.Aggregator

methods.name: tesseract_olap.schema.aggregators.Aggregator.get_params
documentation:
Returns a dict with the serialized params of the instance.

Base class just returns an empty dict. More complex subclasses must
override this method.

Base class for aggregator methods.

The instances contain parameters to compose the associated SQL query.
Backend packages should extend these according to support, and implement
the methods needed for conversion to string.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.schema.aggregators.Quantile
documentation:
Quantile is calculated against the measure's value column.

`quantileExact(quantile_level)(column)`

where quantile_level = Quantile level between 0 and 1

classes.name: tesseract_olap.schema.aggregators.WeightedSum
documentation:
Weighted Sum is calculated against the measure's value column.

`sum(column * weight_column)`

First roll-up is sum(column * weight_column) as weighted_sum_first
Second roll-up is sum(weighted_sum_first) as weighted_sum_final

classes.name: tesseract_olap.schema.aggregators.WeightedAverage
documentation:
Weighted Average is calculated against the measure's value column.

`sum(column * weight_column) / sum(weight_column)`

classes.name: tesseract_olap.schema.aggregators.ReplicateWeightMoe
documentation:
Where the measure column is the primary value, and a list of secondary
columns is provided to the MO aggregator:

The general equation for Margin of Error is

`cv * pow(df * (pow(sum(column) - sum(secondary_columns[0]), 2) + pow(sum(column) - sum(secondary_columns_[1]), 2) + ...), 0.5)`

where cv = critical value, for 90% confidence interval it's 1.645
where df = design factor / #samples

classes.name: tesseract_olap.schema.aggregators.CalculatedMoe
documentation:
Where the moe is already calculated for each row, and this just
aggregates them correctly.

`sqrt(sum(power(moe / cv, 2))) * cv`

where cv = critical value; for 90% confidence interval it's 1.645

classes.name: tesseract_olap.schema.aggregators.WeightedAverageMoe
documentation:
Where the measure column is the primary value,
and a list of secondary weight columns is provided to the MO aggregator:

The general equation for Margin of Error is

`cv * pow(df * (pow(( sum(column * primary_weight)/sum(primary_weight) ) - ( sum(column * secondary_weight_columns[0])/sum(secondary_weight_columns[0]) ), 2) + pow(( sum(column * primary_weight)/sum(primary_weight) ) - ( sum(column * secondary_weight_columns[1]/sum(secondary_weight_columns[1]) ), 2) + ...), 0.5)`

where cv = critical value, for 90% confidence interval it's 1.645
where df = design factor / #samples

Tesseract OLAP server module for LogicLayer.

It must be initialized with a {class}`logiclayer.OlapServer` instance, but
can also be created directly with the schema path and the connection string
using the helper method `TesseractModule.new(connection, schema)`.

modules.name: tesseract_olap.logiclayer
documentation:
Tesseract Module for LogicLayer

This module provides a subclass of {class}`LogicLayerModule`, which provides a
set of endpoints fully compatible with the API of Tesseract Rust.

This submodule it's dependent on the logiclayer package, but as the package it's
not declared as that, it must not be exported in the main module of the package.
The user must import it using

`from tesseract_olap.logiclayer import TesseractModule`.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.logiclayer.dependencies.query_cuts_include

params.name:
Query(
            description="""\
Specifies which values should be considered for the aggregation.

The value is composed by multiple cut definitions separated by semicolons:
    `{value}` := `{cut_def};{cut_def...}`
Where a single cut definition is composed by the Level name, colon, and the list of keys separated by commas:
    `{cut_def}` := `{name}:{key},{key...}`
    `{name}` : `str`, the name of the Level to apply the cut
    `{key}` : `str | int`, the members' ID values

For compatibility, this parameter also accepts the definition of the {name} parameter directly in the URL search params; note the level name must start in caps for this to work. This means both `&include=Year:2009,2010` and `&Year=2009,2010` are equivalent.
"""
        )

documentation:
FastAPI Dependency to parse including cut parameters.

Parses all URL Search Params whose key is capitalized, as cut definitions.
Values are members' IDs, separated by commas.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.logiclayer.dependencies.query_cuts_exclude

params.name:
Query(
            description="""\
Specifies which values should be omitted from the aggregation.

The value is composed by multiple cut definitions separated by semicolons:
    `{value}` := `{cut_def};{cut_def...}`
Where a single cut definition is composed by the Level name, colon, and the list of keys separated by commas:
    `{cut_def}` := `{name}:{key},{key...}`
    `{name}` : `str`, the name of the Level to apply the cut
    `{key}` : `str | int`, the members' ID values
"""
        )

documentation: FastAPI Dependency to parse excluding cut parameters.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.logiclayer.dependencies.query_filters
documentation: FastAPI Dependency to parse filter parameters.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.logiclayer.dependencies.query_parents

params.name:
Query(
            description="""\
Specifies if the request should include the labels for the parent levels of the selected drilldowns.

The shape for the value is:
    `{value}` := `{truthy}` | `{falsey}` | `{name},{name...}`
Where:
    `{truthy}` : A string that can be interpreted as a truthy value; retrieves parents for all drilldowns in the request;
    `{falsey}` : A string that can be interpreted as a falsey value; deactivates parents for all drilldowns in the request (default);
    `{name}` : `str`, the name of the specific drilldown(s) to get parents for.
"""
        )

documentation: FastAPI Dependency to parse parent drilldown call parameters.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.logiclayer.dependencies.query_ranking

params.name:
Query(
            description="""\
Adds a Ranking column to the specified measures.

If unset, or set to a value that can be interpreted as falsey, no rankings are added.
If set to a value that can be interpreted as truthy, a ranking for every measure is added.
Any other string is interpreted as a comma-separated list of the measure names that are expected to be added a ranking.

The value is always an unsigned integer. If many rows have an equal value, all are assigned the same ranking value, and the next value skips the places for the repeated rows.
The direction of the ranking can be specified by (not) prefixing the measure name with a minus character (`-`): the ranking will be calculated in descending order if the minus is present, ascending otherwise.
"""
        )

documentation: FastAPI Dependency to parse ranked results parameter.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.logiclayer.dependencies.dataquery_params

params.name: Query(alias="cube", description="The name of the cube to work with.")

documentation: FastAPI Dependency to parse parameters into a DataRequest object.

functions.name: tesseract_olap.logiclayer.dependencies.membersquery_params

documentation: FastAPI Dependency to parse parameters into a MembersRequest object.

documentation:
Attempts to retrieve a Dimension by its name.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.schema.traverse.CubeTraverser.get_level
documentation:
Attempts to retrieve a Level by its name.

Raises {class}`InvalidEntityName` if the entity can't be found.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.schema.traverse.CubeTraverser.get_time_level
documentation:
Attempts to return a Level from a TIME-type {class}`Dimension` that
matches the name with a time scale name.

Raises {class}`TimeDimensionUnavailable` if the {class}`Cube` doesn't
contain a TIME-type {class}`Dimension`, and {class}`TimeScaleUnavailable`
if the time scale requested is not available in the {class}`Dimension`.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.schema.traverse.CubeTraverser.get_property
documentation:
Attempts to retrieve a Property by its name.

Raises {class}`InvalidEntityName` if the entity can't be found.

Wrapper class for the {class}`Cube` model, that establishes the
relationships between its usages and their source shared entities.

The relationships are made via the {class}`EntityUsageTraverser` subclasses,
initialized upon creation.

classes.name: tesseract_olap.schema.traverse.EntityUsageTraverser

methods.name: tesseract_olap.schema.traverse.EntityUsageTraverser.name
documentation:
Returns the new name given by the usage reference, or the original
name of the entity if not defined.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.schema.traverse.EntityUsageTraverser.annotations
documentation:
Returns a dict containing the combined annotations from the original
entity and its usage, if defined.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.schema.traverse.EntityUsageTraverser.captions
documentation:
Returns a dict containing the combined captions from the original
entity and its usage, if defined.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.schema.traverse.EntityUsageTraverser.get_annotation
documentation:
Retrieves an annotation for the entity.
If the annotation is not defined, raises a {class}`KeyError`.

documentation:
Retrieves the caption of the entity for a certain locale.
If the a caption hasn't been defined for said locale, will attempt to
return the fallback caption, and if not defined either, will return the
entity name.

methods.name: tesseract_olap.schema.traverse.EntityUsageTraverser.get_locale_available
documentation:
Retrieves the list of locales for whose a caption has been defined in
this entity.

Wrapper class to unify an usage with its entity.

Its properties are looked on the usage, then on the entity if not found.
The usage instance is optional, as this wrapper also standardizes the
properties and traversing methods across entities in the codebase.
